sections.description: Parent directory containing all internal packages for the lil-olt-metrics server, organizing functionality into configuration, metric ingestion, storage backends, Prometheus-compatible querying, and an embedded Vue.js SPA dashboard.
sections.purpose: Provides the core business logic and infrastructure for an OpenTelemetry metrics collector that stores metrics in multiple backends, exposes a Prometheus-compatible query API, and serves an embedded Vue.js SPA dashboard for visualizing Claude Code activity metrics.
sections.functionality:
- Routes OTLP metric traffic through gRPC and HTTP handlers into various storage backends (in-memory, filesystem with WAL, Prometheus Remote Write, VictoriaMetrics)
- Exposes a Prometheus query API compatible with PromQL and Grafana, with CORS support and structured HTTP request logging middleware
- Serves an embedded Vue.js SPA at /dashboard/ via Go embed.FS with http.FileServerFS, featuring Chart.js visualizations for Claude Code metrics including token usage, cost tracking, session duration, code activity, and activity heatmaps
- Manages configuration via YAML files with support for environment variable overrides and intelligent duration/size parsing
sections.strengths:
- Well-organized package structure with clear separation of concerns
- Comprehensive test coverage (14+ test files) with integration tests validating end-to-end flows
- Uses interfaces defined at consumption points (config, ingest, query packages depend on store interfaces)
- Code generation via moq for deterministic mocks
- Follows Go best practices: unexported by default, constructor injection, slog structured logging
- Dashboard built as a proper Vue.js SPA with TypeScript, embedded via Go embed.FS for single-binary deployment
sections.weaknesses:
- Query API functionality limited to basic PromQL operations via Prometheus library; custom query extensions require modifying the query package
- Storage adapters (Prometheus Remote, VictoriaMetrics) have integration tests but runtime reliability depends on external services
- Config package has complex YAML unmarshaling logic that could benefit from additional property-based testing
- CORS allows all origins (*) — acceptable for internal tooling but would need tightening for production exposure
sections.test_coverage: 14+ test files across all packages: config/, ingest/, store/, query/, and integration/. Each major package has integration/unit tests. Query package includes tests for CORS headers, OPTIONS preflight handling, dashboard redirect and SPA serving. Tests use t.Parallel(), t.Context(), and slog with io.Discard logger. Uses testify assert/require and moq-generated mocks.
sections.dependencies:
- github.com/prometheus/prometheus (PromQL engine, storage interfaces)
- go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp (OTLP protocol)
- google.golang.org/grpc (gRPC transport)
- gopkg.in/yaml.v3 (config parsing)
- github.com/golang/snappy (compression)
- embed (Go embed.FS for dashboard SPA dist)
- io/fs (fs.Sub for serving embedded SPA)
- Internal: config provides TranslationConfig and ByteSize/Duration types; ingest depends on store.Appender and store.Store interfaces; query depends on storage.Queryable interface; all storage backends implement store.Store
sections.files:
No direct .go files in internal/ root. Subdirectories:

| Directory | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| config/ | YAML config loading, duration/bytesize parsing |
| ingest/ | gRPC/HTTP OTLP handlers, metric translation |
| store/ | Store/Appender interfaces, MemStore, FSStore with WAL, Prometheus Remote, VictoriaMetrics adapters |
| query/ | Prometheus-compatible HTTP API, embedded Vue.js SPA dashboard served via embed.FS, dashboard/dist/ contains Vite build output |
| integration/ | End-to-end tests |
| tools/ | Tool version pinning |
